Metric: Software name? (string)
URL? (URL)
Affiliation (institution(s)) (string or N/A)
Response: Inria (France); four active Inria teams: Mimesis, Imagine, Defrost, Asclepios; founded 2006 by Inria alongside USTL, UJF, CNRS, and MGH (Massachusetts General Hospital)

Metric: Software purpose (string)
Response: Open-source real-time medical simulation and interactive simulation framework; provides component-based architecture for deformable object simulation, finite element methods, collision detection, and haptic feedback; primary applications in surgical simulation, medical education, and soft robotics

Software Category? The concept category includes software that does not have an officially released version. Public software has a released version in the public domain. Private software has a released version available to authorized users only. (concept, public, private)
Development model? (open source, freeware, commercial, unclear)
Publications about the software? Refers to publications that have used or mentioned the software. (number or unknown)
Source code URL? (set of url, n/a, unclear)
Programming language(s)? (set of FORTRAN, Matlab, C, C++, Java, R, Ruby, Python, Cython, BASIC, Pascal, IDL, unclear, other∗) ∗ given via string
Is there evidence that performance was considered? Performance refers to either speed, storage, or throughput. (yes∗, no)
Additional comments? (can cover any metrics you feel are missing, or any other thoughts you have)
Response: SOFA is the leading open-source framework for medical simulation. Component-based scene graph architecture allows mixing and matching deformable models, collision algorithms, and solvers. SofaPython3 plugin provides Python scripting API. Used in surgical simulation, catheter navigation planning, and soft robot control. Plugin ecosystem (SOFA-Framework plugin store) extends functionality significantly.

Response: Prebuilt binary makes installation straightforward. Building SofaPython3 plugin from source requires additional steps. SOFA's GUI and scene-graph paradigm has a learning curve distinct from other simulation tools reviewed.

Response: Component-based architecture is highly reusable. Plugin system enables extension without modifying core. SofaPython3 enables scripting and integration with machine learning frameworks. LGPL core allows commercial use with open plugin sharing.
